item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea of being itchy forever in places you can't phyisically scratch was sufficiently disturbing for me to stand on its own. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simple doesn't mean bad. Not every article can be a grand maratonic piece. We would go crazy. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sounds like something MC&D would LOVE to get their hands on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have some kind of foot fungus that means that sometimes I get very, very itchy with little recourse. It's not a lot more enjoyable to imagine than it is to go through it.

This wasn't entertaining-uncomfortable, it was just normal uncomfortable. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This doesn't work for me. It's well-written stylistically, but unless I do the SCP a lot of favors and speculate on how the effects will feel long-term and envision the kids in question, etc, this is flatly dull. And the SCP should be encouraging that more for me with all kinds of literary tricks at our disposal. But it doesn't; the birthday party was a good idea, but it's simply not developed at all. It's generic, and I don't care about generic.

I felt uncomfortable only after reading the comment thread, and that's not enough for me.

-1, but a rewrite could likely win me over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now I'm itchy. Forever.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
didn't notice proprioception in that list of affected nerve types. Dunno how that works, but… I bet it would suck

Affected people are literally itchy in a place that doesn't exist, so they can't scratch it. Simply being aware of where your hand is is itchy. Movement itself is itchy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Short and simple, and I think going beyond "you get itchy" to "every sensation you feel is itch" gives the reader something to think about.

Edit: didn't notice proprioception in that list of affected nerve types. Dunno how that works, but… I bet it would suck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What I like about this article is that on the surface, it's pretty boring. It's written well, don't get me wrong, but it's like reading an actual medical document. Dry. That's not a bad thing, especially with how horrifying the actual effects of the skip are. I liked having to do a mental double take. I feel like this is how SCP documents would look like if the Foundation was real.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Short and to the point, doesn't work for every article but I like it here. I feel the same way Cyclopian does in that this feels like a Dr. Wondertainment product, it would just be tricky to include that without it feeling forced. It would work for me with or without the Dr. Wondertainment connection, it's up to the author.

I think not knowing what becomes of the people after they're effected helps as well. The article seems to imply they're alive but restrained, probably driven mad by the itching.

Up vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something a kid would find incredibly awesome but with (seemingly accidental on the part of the creator) horrific consequences? This seems like something Dr. Wondertainment would do. You don't need to include it but it would work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do- do they ever recover? Christ, this is horrifying. I want to give these people a hug, but I'm afraid I'll make it worse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was too bare-bones to work for me. No pun intended.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seems like if this was a professionally-developed object, it'd be much better marketed as a non-lethal self-defense device than as a kid's toy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's kind of simple and unremarkable, but it works. I didn't see any real issues, except perhaps what happens later with "infected" people. I assume that they will ultimately die, but it's likely that they could live if restrained, but be driven insane from the itching.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This seems like it could easily be another project by Phobos Labs, the GOI find of 2015.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's very plain to the point of boring. However, the thought of nine-year olds literally scratching themselves down the bone made me upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's good. Anomalies can't always be elaborate. Sometimes reality is really mundane.

I'm feeling a light to decent +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not entirely sure where you were going with this. I like the idea of a horrific "practical joke" SCP, but there's no hook or meat to this for me aside from "it's a gun what makes itchy water."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Slight reworking of a SCP I posted a while back. Hopefully it'll fly better this time.

Also? Worst. Birthday party. Ever.
